summary: codebuff-cli typosquats CodebuffAI's `codebuff` and drops an unverified binary from a personal GitHub account at postinstall
details: The package name `codebuff-cli` is a one-token edit from the legitimate `codebuff` package by CodebuffAI, and the README reproduces CodebuffAI's branding and instructs users to `npm install -g codebuff` — strong evidence of intentional name confusion. On install, `cli/scripts/download-binary.cjs` fetches a platform-specific executable from `https://github.com/Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli/releases/download/v<version>/...` (a personal GitHub user, unrelated to CodebuffAI), writes it to `~/.codebuff/<binary>`, and chmods it `0755` with no hash or signature verification. The publisher of the dropped binary does not match the publisher implied by the README, the URL is not integrity-checked, and the bytes that end up executable on the installer's machine are entirely under the personal account's control and can be changed at any time. This is the typosquat + dropper combination: name confusion induces installation, and the postinstall hook gives the attacker an arbitrary-code path on the installer's system.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
